title: Demo & Watch - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand the platform-token tier: install demo and watch.

questions.title: What does `dtwiz install demo` deploy?
questions.content: What does `dtwiz install demo --experimental --yes` set up?

options.text: The schnitzel demo app (a multi-service Python app), instrumented with OpenTelemetry and sending telemetry to your tenant
options.explanation: The demo installer downloads schnitzel into ./schnitzel/, instruments it and starts it.

options.text: A Kubernetes cluster running a Todo app
options.explanation: No Kubernetes involved - the demo runs as local Python processes.

options.text: A Dynatrace OneAgent on the container host
options.explanation: That would be `dtwiz install oneagent` - the demo uses OpenTelemetry instead.

questions.explanation: install demo = download schnitzel + OpenTelemetry instrumentation + start, pointed at your DT_ENVIRONMENT.
hints: Look at the ./schnitzel/ directory it creates.
Which signal type arrives - OneAgent traces or OTel spans?

questions.title: Why do demo and watch need a different token?
questions.content: `dtwiz install demo` and `dtwiz watch` refused to run with the lab's access token. Why?

options.text: They use the Dynatrace platform APIs, which require a platform token (dt0s16) via DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN
options.explanation: Classic dt0c01 tokens cover the classic APIs (status, recommend, most installs) but not the platform data APIs.
GitHub is only involved when downloading dtwiz itself.
The same token kept working for recommend - it is a token TYPE difference, not expiry.

questions.explanation: demo + watch talk to platform APIs - that requires DT_PLATFORM_TOKEN (dt0s16). dtwiz tells you exactly this when the token is missing.
hints: Remember the token-type info box in Section 02.

questions.title: Why the --experimental flag?
questions.content: Why does the demo install require `--experimental`?

options.text: The demo installer is an experimental dtwiz feature - the flag is an explicit opt-in
options.explanation: Experimental features (demo install, Docker/OTel-update recommendations) are gated behind the flag.

options.text: It enables experimental Dynatrace tenant features
options.explanation: The flag changes nothing on the tenant - it gates dtwiz's own experimental functionality.

questions.explanation: --experimental opts into dtwiz's experimental features; --yes handles the prompts.

learningObjectives: Know which token unlocks the platform tier.
